list.title: module:ApiQueueTask.processNewChildren
list.description: Read all unprocessed children (ApiResultNodes appended by fetchRes callers), extract their request AAs, add to the internal FIFO queue, and dispatch. Periodically prunes processed children to prevent unbounded accumulation.

list.title: module:ApiQueueTask.runQueueLoop
list.description:
FIFO request queue coordinator (Architecture C — continuous-server).

Receives ApiResultNode children appended by fetchRes() callers. Each node carries the request AA on its 'request' field. The coordinator reads ALL unprocessed children when woken up, dispatches each to the first free ApiTask pool slot, then routes the completed response back to the ApiResultNode so the orchestrator unblocks.

Why children instead of a shared field: SceneGraph can coalesce port events when multiple Task threads write to the same field in rapid succession — the coordinator would see fewer events than writes, silently losing requests. Children are immune: even if wakeup events coalesce, the coordinator reads ALL pending children from the node tree, so no request is ever lost regardless of timing.

Startup ordering guarantee:

  1. Each ApiTask pool slot sets isReady=true after registering its request observer.
  2. This coordinator waits for ALL pool slots to be ready before registering its own observers and setting isReady=true.
  3. fetchRes() waits for this coordinator's isReady=true before appending children. Result: pool observers → coordinator ready → fetchRes appends. No startup race.

Thread safety: Only this task thread reads/writes m.queue, m.inFlight, and m.processedIndex — no TOCTOU race. Child appends from other threads go through SceneGraph rendezvous.

list.title: module:ApiQueueTask.tryDispatch
list.description: Dispatch queued requests to free pool slots (FIFO). Only ever called from this coordinator's task thread — no concurrent access.

list.title: module:ApiTask.executeRequest
list.description: Executes a single HTTP request via roku-requests.

list.title: module:ApiTask.runApiLoop
list.description: Continuous-server event loop (Architecture C). Waits for request field writes, executes HTTP via roku-requests, writes response. Never exits — lives for the lifetime of the app alongside the global pool.

list.title: module:AudioPlayerView.applyTrackInfo
list.description: Apply the queue-derived now-playing display (track number, title, artist, song, total length) from the current item. Called SYNCHRONOUSLY on a track change (pageContentChanged) so these update on the same tick as the position-bar/timestamp reset instead of lagging until the async stream load returns, and re-applied from the async stream/metadata callbacks once a fuller item resolves (idempotent — setFieldTextValue/setScreenTitle no-op when unchanged or invalid). The poster/backdrop stay in the async path: image loads are inherently async.

list.title: module:AudioPlayerView.handleTransport
list.description:
handleTransport: Roku voice transport handler. Called from source/main.bs when an roInputEvent with info.type = "transport" arrives and AudioPlayerView is the active scene.

Returns { status: "" } per roInput.EventResponse(). Status codes mirror video.

JellyRock-specific behaviors (audio has no continuous trickplay, no media segments):

  • "forward"/"rewind" are discrete 10s seeks.
  • "replay" seeks backward by playbackInstantReplaySeconds (same setting as video) — matches the Roku platform spec; users wanting loop control use the "loop" command.
  • "skip" advances to the next track.

See https://github.com/rokudev/dev-doc — docs/DEVELOPER/media-playback/voice-controls/transport-controls.md

list.description: Stop + tear down without navigating (a deep link arrived while audio plays; the caller reroutes to the new content). Stops the audio the same way the back key does (onKeyEvent "back" -> control "stop") — the caller's goBack is programmatic, so it bypasses that key handler. NOT a full onDestroy: the caller's goBack triggers onDestroy for cleanup, and running onDestroy twice (it nulls m.audioPlayer) crashes the second pass.

list.title: module:AuthManager.canActivate
list.description:
canActivate guard — see AuthManager.xml header. Invoked by the router on the render thread during navigateTo, once per guarded route. A cheap SYNCHRONOUS token check only — no network (launch-time AboutMe re-validation stays in the login flow).

currentRequest is the route object the router is resolving; currentRequest.path is the full requested path (incl. any query string), which is exactly what we stash so the post-login replay can resume the original destination.

Returns: true — token present: allow the navigation. { path: "/login" } — no token: stash the requested path for post-login replay and redirect to /login. The router treats any AA with a non-empty path as a redirect command (Router.brs sgrouter_runGuardChecks) — the same shape sgrouter.createRedirectCommand("/login") produces, without pulling the whole router namespace into this guard node.

Invoked by the presenter via callFunc so the body runs in component-m context, where fetchAsync + auto-abandon work — the presenter is a class, and the auto-abandon plugin only injects into components with onDestroy (which BaseGridView has). The presenter still owns m.apiFilters + the toast; this just fetches and hands the result back via deliverFilters().

A missing context node is therefore an unexpected route shape, not a deferred feature — warn rather than silently render nothing.

list.title: module:ExtrasRowList.loadParts
list.description:
loadParts: Start the extras loading chain appropriate for the item type.

Chain by type: Movie / Video / Recording → Chapters → AdditionalParts → People → LikeThis → SpecialFeatures Episode → Chapters → SeasonEpisodes → People → LikeThis MusicVideo → People → LikeThis Series → Seasons → People → LikeThis Season → Episodes ("Season X") → People → LikeThis BoxSet → BoxSetItems ("Movies") → People → LikeThis MusicArtist → Albums → Appears On → Songs → ArtistSimilar Photo → LikeThis PhotoAlbum → Album Photos → LikeThis TvChannel → Upcoming Programs → LikeThis Program → More on Channel → LikeThis m.top.type must be set before calling this function (done by ShowScenes.bs).

list.description: onArtistSongsLoaded: Build "Songs" row then chain to ArtistSimilar (MusicArtist only). Uses ArtistIds (broad) so songs on compilation albums surface even when album-level metadata doesn't associate the artist via AlbumArtistIds.

list.title: module:ExtrasRowList.onProgramsExpired
list.description:
Fires when JRRowList's progress tick detects at least one expired Program among this list's rows. Triggers a targeted refetch of the Channel Programs row (TvChannel "Up Next" or Program "More on this Channel") so finished broadcasts roll off and new ones take their place.

Deliberately does NOT re-chain to LikeThisTask — related content is stable over a viewing session and should not be refetched just because a program expired. m.isRefetchingChannelPrograms debounces repeated expiry signals.

list.title: module:ExtrasRowList.onSeasonsLoaded
list.description: onSeasonsLoaded: Build "Seasons" row then chain to People (Series only)

list.title: module:ExtrasRowList.populateRow
list.description: populateRow: Reuse an existing row ContentNode (clearing its children) or create a new one. Creating appends the row to m.top.content at the current end — correct since the async chain fires in display order. m.rowHeights is reset at chain start and rebuilt here on every call, keeping it in sync with m.top.content across refreshes where rows may appear or disappear. Returns the row for the caller to store as a named ref.

list.title: module:ExtrasRowList.removeRow
list.description: removeRow: Remove a row ContentNode from content and keep numRows in sync with content.getChildCount(). Always call this instead of m.top.content.removeChild() directly; then null the caller's ref.

list.title: module:ExtrasRowList.updatePanelPosition
list.description:
updatePanelPosition: Set targetTranslationY so ItemDetails.bs can animate the RowList's translation within extrasGrp. extrasGrp stays fixed at extrasLayout.PANEL_OPEN_Y (306).

numRows always equals the total row count (set in populateRow), so floatingFocus never scrolls internally. All rows stay at fixed RowList-relative Y positions: row 0 → RowList Y = 0 row N → RowList Y = sum(heights[0..N-1]) + N * m.rowSpacing (= focusedRowY)

Visible screen height between panel top and action zone: extrasLayout.ACTION_SAFE_BOTTOM_Y - extrasLayout.PANEL_OPEN_Y = 720px.

Three position classes: TOP — row 0 (or only row): focused row at RowList Y=0, default translation (18px). BOTTOM — last row: focused row bottom edge at action zone - EXTRAS_BOTTOM_SAFE_PADDING. extrasLayout.PANEL_OPEN_Y + translationY + focusedRowY + rowHeight = extrasLayout.ACTION_SAFE_BOTTOM_Y - EXTRAS_BOTTOM_SAFE_PADDING translationY = (extrasLayout.ACTION_SAFE_BOTTOM_Y - extrasLayout.PANEL_OPEN_Y - EXTRAS_BOTTOM_SAFE_PADDING) - rowHeight - focusedRowY MIDDLE — rows 1..N-2: focused row centered in the visible area. translationY = int((extrasLayout.ACTION_SAFE_BOTTOM_Y - extrasLayout.PANEL_OPEN_Y - rowHeight) / 2) - focusedRowY

Negative translationY moves the RowList above extrasGrp Y=0. The clippingRect Group in ExtrasSlider.xml clips that overflow so the previous row only peeks within the panel bounds. targetTranslationY is observed by ItemDetails.bs which runs the gridAnime Animation. Called early from onKeyEvent (before RowList focuses the row) and confirmed by onRowItemFocused.

list.title: module:GridItem.evaluateGridTextureState
list.description:
Central texture decision — called when renderTracking, textureManagerState, or loadedRowRange changes.

State machine (same as JRRowItem but without horizontal buffer): "destroyed" → force unload everything (no guards) "init" → no-op (freeze state during layout changes) "hidden" → no-op (freeze — screen is behind another on the stack) "active" → managed rows (visible + buffer): always loaded. Outside rows: renderTracking decides.

list.title: module:GridItem.forceUnloadGridTexture
list.description: Force-unloads textures unconditionally — used only during onDestroy(). Ignores m.isTextureUnloaded and m.cachedPosterUri guards so every cell releases memory.

list.title: module:GridItem.getGridRowPosition
list.description: Returns this cell's row position relative to the managed row ranges. Derives the grid row from the cached flat index and numColumns. or "outside" if not in any managed range

list.title: module:GridItem.getGridTextureManagerState
list.description: Returns the current texture manager state from the content root.

list.title: module:GridItem.onFocusChanged
list.description: Enable title scrolling based on item focus

list.title: module:GridItem.onItemContentChanged
list.description: Re-render when the parent assigns a new item to this (possibly recycled) cell. Texture observers only need itemContent, so wire them up even before the cell has been sized; the actual render waits until width/height are known (onSizeChanged fires the first paint when the parent applies itemSize/rowItemSize).

list.title: module:GridItem.onLoadedRowRangeChanged
list.description: Fires when the parent screen updates the loaded row range (focus change).

list.title: module:GridItem.onPosterLoadStatusChanged
list.description: Toggle the JRPlaceholder fallback based on the real poster's load state. Mirrors the canonical state machine from JRRowItem.bs onPosterLoadStatusChanged.

list.title: module:GridItem.onRenderTrackingChanged
list.description: Fires when the cell scrolls in/out of the visible area.

list.title: module:GridItem.onSizeChanged
list.description: Re-render when the parent applies/changes the cell size (MarkupGrid itemSize or RowList rowItemSize). Mirrors JRRowItem so one component renders every cell size.

list.title: module:GridItem.onTextureManagerStateChanged
list.description: Fires when textureManagerState changes (init → active, active → hidden, etc.)

list.title: module:GridItem.reloadGridTexture
list.description: Restores poster URI from cache when the cell scrolls back on screen. The backdrop will show briefly while the image reloads from Roku's HTTP cache.

list.title: module:GridItem.renderGenreTile
list.description: Renders a "View All [Genre]" action tile: themed backdrop + centered genre label, no poster image. The below-tile item title is hidden — the tile's own centered label already names the genre, and the row's genre header sits directly above it, so a third copy is redundant. cachedPosterUri is cleared so texture management skips this cell.

list.title: module:GridItem.renderMediaItem
list.description: Renders a standard media item: poster image with the JRPlaceholder fallback state machine and texture management.

list.title: module:GridItem.resetPlaceholderToLoading
list.description: Reset the JRPlaceholder to loading state (themed backdrop visible, no glyph). Used by the initial-load branches in renderMediaItem and by the texture unload paths so off-screen / unloaded cells don't carry a stale failure-state glyph back into view when they reload.

list.title: module:GridItem.resolveGridViewConfig
list.description: Resolves the owning BaseGridView and reads its display/title config. Walks up the node tree to the first ancestor exposing gridTitles rather than assuming a fixed depth: the MarkupGrid puts cells 2 levels below BaseGridView, but the genre RowList nests them deeper. The old fixed GetParent().GetParent() walk missed BaseGridView for RowList cells, so genre tiles silently fell back to scaleToZoom (cropping posters the presenter intended as scaleToFit) and an empty gridTitles (no titles ever). Idempotent — caches m.gridView on first success.

list.title: module:GridItem.resolveLoadDisplayMode
list.description: Picks the poster scaling mode. Music posters are square art that must not be cropped; everything else uses the grid's configured display mode (default scaleToZoom).

list.title: module:GridItem.setPosterRect
list.description: Positions/sizes the placeholder, poster, and genre backdrop to the same rect.

list.title: module:GridItem.setupGridTextureObserver
list.description: One-time setup: observes loadedRowRange and textureManagerState on the MarkupGrid's content root. The content root is the direct parent of itemContent in the content tree (flat model, unlike RowList's 2-level hierarchy): contentRoot → itemNode (itemContent) Called from onItemContentChanged. Skips if already connected.

list.title: module:GridItem.shouldLoadGridTexture
list.description: Determines whether this cell should load its poster during initial render. Mirrors evaluateGridTextureState logic: managed rows trust buffer logic, outside rows trust renderTracking.

list.title: module:GridItem.showPlaceholder
list.description: Flip the placeholder into failure state with a type-appropriate glyph, then clear the poster URI so the glyph isn't covered by a stale broken image. itemType="" puts JRPlaceholder in loading state (backdrop only, no glyph); resolveItemPlaceholderType returns "" only for invalid/typeless items, so valid items always end up here with a typed glyph or the Folder fallback.

list.title: module:GridItem.unloadGridTexture
list.description: Clears poster URI to release texture memory when the cell is off-screen. Items with no real image (cachedPosterUri = "") are skipped — they already show a lightweight local placeholder that costs negligible texture memory.

list.title: module:GridItem.updateLayout
list.description: Sizes and positions every child to the current cell dimensions. The focus slot is rowItemSize/itemSize; the poster fills it at POSTER_TOP_OFFSET so the poster overflows the slot bottom and the focus border (drawn behind it) reads with a top margin and no overlap. The title sits below at POSTER_TOP_OFFSET + h + TITLE_GAP — kept OUTSIDE the focus border because the container sets rowHeights/itemSize taller than the slot (the focus border pins to the slot, not the cell's full bounding box).

Results arrive via onFiltersLoaded().

list.title: module:Home.handleFocus
list.description: Router focus hook (overrides JRScreen.handleFocus). The router asks the resuming view to take focus right after onViewResume -> onScreenShown. The base implementation uses restoreFocus(m.top), which focuses a Home row and clobbers the overhang-icon (gear/search) focus onScreenShown just set. Re-run Home's overhang-aware restore so the gear stays focused on return (matching prod), then consume the one-shot overhang target.

list.title: module:Home.handleHomeMenuAction
list.description: Route a Home menu action. Navigation actions resolve through the router on the render thread; session-ending actions (change user/server, sign out) are emitted as userMenuAction for main.bs (which owns the LoginFlow re-entry via goto appStart).

list.title: module:Home.initVoiceBox
list.description: initVoiceBox: One-time voice setup in init() — matches BaseGridView's pattern. Sets voiceEnabled=true ONCE and never sets it back to false. The firmware silently manages voiceEnabled when other screens claim voice routing. Only active is toggled on screen show/hide.

list.title: module:Home.loadLibraries

list.title: module:Home.onCloseSidePanel

list.title: module:Home.onDestroy

list.title: module:Home.onIconFocusReturn
list.description: Overhang icon signals DOWN was pressed — return focus to active content

list.title: module:Home.onOverhangIconAction
list.description: Overhang icon (search/settings) was pressed — proxy to Home's interface for Main.bs. Saves the focused icon for focus restoration on return, then moves focus to Home group BEFORE firing the action. This ensures the router's suspend saves a focus inside Home's tree. We focus m.top (not activeContent) to avoid a visible focus jump to the row list before the screen transition.

list.title: module:Home.onQuickPlayLaunch
list.description: Forward a fallen-through item tap to the playback launcher.

list.title: module:Home.onQuickPlayNode
list.description: Proxy quickPlayNode from whichever row list fires it. Must pass through invalid clears so Home.quickPlayNode doesn't retain a stale value that re-fires the m.port observer on screen restoration.

list.title: module:Home.onRowItemSelected
list.description: A row item was selected — navigate to its route, passing the rich content node as context (so details/library self-load without a re-fetch). Items that aren't view navigation (e.g. Chapter) fall through to the playback path via quickPlayNode.

list.title: module:Home.onScreenShown

list.title: module:Home.onSidePanelOption
list.description: Options side-panel button pressed — close the panel, then route the action.

list.title: module:Home.onTabBarFocusReturn
list.description: Tab bar signals DOWN was pressed — return focus to active content

list.title: module:Home.onTabChanged
list.description: Called when selectedTabId changes (proxied back from the overhang tab bar by JRScene's overhang controller). Destroys the old tab's content and creates the new tab's content.

list.title: module:Home.onUserDropdownFocusReturn
list.description: User dropdown signals DOWN was pressed — return focus to active content

list.title: module:Home.onUserMenuAction
list.description: User dropdown menu item was selected — proxy to Home's interface for Main.bs. Saves the dropdown so onScreenShown can restore focus when the user returns, then moves focus into Home's tree so the router's suspend saves a focus that back-from-route can restore.

list.title: module:Home.onVoiceSearch
list.description: onVoiceSearch: Fires when voice input is captured by the hidden VoiceTextEditBox. Signals Main.bs to open SearchResults with the spoken query pre-populated.

list.title: module:Home.restoreHomeFocus
list.description:
Restore Home's focus, OVERHANG-ICON-AWARE. Priority:

  1. The overhang element that triggered the last navigation (gear / search / dropdown). These live in the persistent overhang — a child of JRScene, NOT of Home — so they can't be reached via m.top.lastFocus.
  2. Home's own lastFocus (normal in-group restoration), skipping the hidden voice box.
  3. Active content, then the Home group. Called by BOTH onScreenShown and handleFocus: the router invokes handleFocus right after onScreenShown, and its base restoreFocus(m.top) would otherwise override the overhang-icon focus with a Home row (the "gear focus removed on return to Home" regression). Does NOT clear the one-shot m.lastOverhangFocus — handleFocus clears it once the resume's focus restore is complete.

list.title: module:HomeRows.buildSectionPlan
list.description: buildSectionPlan: Reads homeSection0-6 settings, returns ordered array of sections to display

list.title: module:HomeRows.cleanupLatestMediaTasks

list.description: createSkeletonRow: Creates a single empty HomeRow with correct title, sectionId, and cursorSize. Adds a single placeholder child node so the RowList renders the row.

list.title: module:HomeRows.findLatestMediaInsertIndex

list.description: findRowBySectionId: Find a row in content by its sectionId field

list.title: module:HomeRows.getItemAtIndices
list.description: Gets an item from content at specified row and item indices Performs all necessary bounds checking and validation

list.title: module:HomeRows.getRowConfigForSection
list.description: getRowConfigForSection: Returns title and slotSize for a sectionId. Used when inserting a row that was previously removed (no skeleton exists).

list.title: module:HomeRows.getSectionIdForType
list.description: getSectionIdForType: Maps a section type string to its sectionId

list.title: module:HomeRows.insertLatestMediaSkeletons
list.description: insertLatestMediaSkeletons: Creates skeleton rows for each non-excluded library and inserts them at the correct position in the content node.

list.title: module:HomeRows.loadLibraries
list.description: loadLibraries: Entry point called by Home.bs via callFunc. Builds ordered section plan, creates skeleton rows, then fires data tasks.

list.title: module:HomeRows.onDestroy

list.title: module:HomeRows.onItemFocused
list.description: Observer for rowItemFocused field - delegates to updateBackdropForFocusedItem

list.description: onLibrariesLoaded: Handler when LoadLibrariesTask returns data. Populates the library row, creates latestmedia skeleton rows, then fires latest tasks.

list.title: module:HomeRows.onProgramsExpired
list.description: Fires when JRRowList's progress tick detects at least one expired Program or Recording. Re-runs LoadOnNowTask and/or LoadActiveRecordingsTask to pull fresh data. Loading guards debounce repeated expiry signals while a load is already in flight, and section plan checks avoid wasted requests when the user has disabled the relevant sections.

list.title: module:HomeRows.onSectionPopulated
list.description: onSectionPopulated: Called after each section is populated with data. Signals AppLaunchComplete after 2 sections have loaded.

list.title: module:HomeRows.populateLibraryRow
list.description: populateLibraryRow: Fills the library row with filtered library items. Uses populateRowFromData for consistent in-place update behavior.

list.title: module:HomeRows.populateRowFromData
list.description: populateRowFromData: Unified row population logic used by all update callbacks. Updates the children of the existing row node in place to avoid RowList re-layout and focus disruption. Removes the row if data is empty. If the row doesn't exist but data is available (e.g., a previously empty section now has content on refresh), creates and inserts the row at the correct position.

list.title: module:HomeRows.removeRowAtIndex
list.description: removeRowAtIndex: Removes a row at the given index and recalculates sizes

list.title: module:HomeRows.setRowItemSize
list.description: setRowItemSize: Loops through all home sections and sets the correct item sizes, heights, and spacings per row. rowItemSize[i] = slot size [width, posterHeight] — determines focus ring dimensions (poster only, no text). rowHeights[i] = total row height: slot + 90px text area for standard rows; slot-only for library tiles. rowSpacings[i] = gap after each row before the next row label. Must be set for ALL rows because Roku ignores itemSpacing entirely once rowSpacings is assigned (even partially). Standard rows use 60px; My Media uses 78px to partially compensate for its absent text area.

list.title: module:HomeRows.startLatestMediaLoads

list.title: module:HomeRows.startParallelLoads

list.title: module:HomeRows.updateHomeRows

list.title: module:HomeRows.updateLatestItems

list.title: module:IconButton.defaultIconSize
list.description: Spinner rendered smaller than 64px icon frame — icons have transparent padding so this matches the visible icon area

list.title: module:IconButton.focusBorderOffset
list.description: Used for positioning calculations only (matches 6px border in 9-patch image); does NOT control actual border rendering

list.title: module:IconButton.onLoadingChanged

list.title: module:IconButton.spinnerSize
list.description: Text extends 6px on each side to prevent label wrapping

list.title: module:IconButton.textExtension
list.description: Used for positioning calculations only (matches 6px border in 9-patch image); does NOT control actual border rendering

list.title: module:IconButton.updateButtonPositioning

list.title: module:IntegerKeyboard.enableVoiceWithVisibility
list.description: Voice follows visibility. Roku allows only ONE voice-enabled VoiceTextEditBox on screen at a time. sgRouter mounts a view hidden then reveals it (visible=false->true); revealing >1 voice-enabled box at once faults Roku's native voice arbiter (the Settings-open crash, since Settings mounts several keyboards). Keeping voice enabled only while THIS keyboard is shown means at most one is ever voice-enabled — no contention — while preserving voice dictation when the keyboard is actually on screen. (SceneManager mounted views already-visible, so the boxes registered incrementally and never tripped this; the router's bulk reveal exposed it.)

list.title: module:ItemDetails.ESTIMATED_BUTTON_ROW_HEIGHT_PX
list.description: First-frame estimate for the button row's total rendered height. Used by anchorDateLabel() before boundingRect() has settled (it reports height=0 during initial render). Observed to land around 130 px for the typical 2-line-label icon button. A later renderTracking callback replaces this with the real measured height once layout completes.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.ITEM_DETAILS_EXTRAS_PADDING

list.title: module:ItemDetails.LOGO_MAX_DISPLAY_HEIGHT

list.title: module:ItemDetails.LOGO_MAX_DISPLAY_WIDTH
list.description: Maximum display width for the logo image — prevents very wide/flat logos from overlapping buttons

list.title: module:ItemDetails.LOGO_MIN_DISPLAY_HEIGHT
list.description: Minimum display height for the logo image — images too small are scaled up (aspect ratio preserved).

list.title: module:ItemDetails.SetDefaultAudioTrack
list.description: SetDefaultAudioTrack: Auto-select the best audio stream based on user language prefs, storing the result on m.top.selectedAudioStreamIndex. The dropdown trigger label is refreshed separately by applyDropdown() using the same index.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.anchorDateLabel
list.description: anchorDateLabel: Derive the date label's Y position from the button group's position. Returns dateLabelY so callers (e.g. logo positioning) can use it without duplicating the math. Safe to call multiple times — idempotent, and safe to call BEFORE the button group has finished laying out: falls back to the group's fixed translation + ESTIMATED_BUTTON_ROW_HEIGHT_PX so the label never lands off-screen. A later renderTracking callback refines the position once the real rendered height is known.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.applyDropdown
list.description: applyDropdown: Push items + selection into a single TrackDropdown. Hides the slot when the track type isn't applicable (keeps layout order stable for the other two slots).

list.title: module:ItemDetails.buildAudioDropdownItems
list.description: buildAudioDropdownItems: Convert the MediaStreams array into dropdown items for audio. Item id is the stringified server-side stream index, matching the ItemDetails contract that selectedAudioStreamIndex is an integer spanning all stream types.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.buildSubtitleDropdownItems
list.description:
buildSubtitleDropdownItems: Convert the MediaStreams array into dropdown items for subtitles.

Two shapes are returned depending on whether the item actually has subtitle streams: No subtitle streams at all → [{ id:"-1", title:"None" }]. Rendered as a static non-interactive slot so the "Subtitles" title row still shows and the layout stays consistent across items. "None" (not "Off") because the user has no choice to make — subtitles simply don't exist on this item. One or more subtitle streams → [{ id:"-1", title:"Off" }, ...tracks]. The "Off" entry lets the user disable subtitles when tracks ARE present. id "-1" is the SubtitleSelection.NONE sentinel used by m.top.selectedSubtitleStreamIndex.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.buildVideoDropdownItems
list.description: buildVideoDropdownItems: Convert MediaSources into dropdown items. "Video" here is the MediaSource, not a stream — it's the user-facing alternate-version selector (e.g. "1080p H264" vs "4K HEVC HDR10").

list.title: module:ItemDetails.checkDeepLinkLaunch
list.description: See the call site in onScreenShown. A deep-link route carries ?deeplink= (open | play | shuffle | trailer | instantmix). We handle it once per distinct navigation (route.id), so a repeat cast of an item whose ItemDetails is cached (keepAlive) still fires, while an ordinary back-from-player resume (which keeps the same route.id) does not re-fire. The ACTION (the sender's explicit intent — never inferred from the resolved item) is dispatched in dispatchDeepLinkAction. Fresh/loading -> flag for onDetailsLoaded (which also handles an invalid id -> error + Home); already-loaded resume -> dispatch once the router idles.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.checkTrailerAvailability
list.description: Fetches whether the item has local trailers, to add/remove the Trailer button (onTrailerAvailableChanged reacts to the result). Render-thread promise — replaces the former trailerResultNode + onTrailerCheckDone observer (Phase-3b: a render-thread submitApiRequest + observeField flow → fetchAsync().then(); canonical in docs/dev/promises.md).

list.title: module:ItemDetails.createDividerNode
list.description: createDividerNode: Create a bullet divider node for separating info items

list.title: module:ItemDetails.createEndsAtNode
list.description: createEndsAtNode: Build an "Ends at" time label with AM/PM support and store it on m.endsAtNode for dynamic clock updates. Used by types with runTimeTicks-based durations.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.createInfoLabel
list.description: createInfoLabel: Create a bold label node for the info rows

list.title: module:ItemDetails.createTimeLabel
list.description: createTimeLabel: Creates a time label with an optional lowercase am/pm suffix. In 24h mode returns a single label; in 12h mode appends the period to the time text.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.deferDeepLinkLaunchUntilIdle
list.description: keepAlive resume: the content is already loaded, but onScreenShown runs MID-navigation, and the router rejects a navigateTo issued while a navigation is in progress (Router.brs _navigateTo). So wait for the navigation to settle (routerState -> NavigationEnd) before launching, otherwise startPlayFromDetail's navigateTo("/play") is swallowed (toast fires, but no player). Fresh mounts don't need this — they launch from onDetailsLoaded, which is already async / router-idle.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.dispatchDeepLinkAction
list.description: Do exactly what the route's action asked, on the validated item. The action is the SENDER'S explicit intent — never inferred from the resolved item: open (and any unknown action) -> stay on the springboard (this view). Grid-container targets (library/folder/genre/studio/channel) never reach here — JRScene.onDeepLinkResolved diverts them to their grid. play -> QueueManager.launchItem (the DRY per-type play engine: video resumes its bookmark, Series smart-resumes, containers play, live watches, audio -> audio player, photo -> viewer). shuffle -> the same path the Shuffle button uses (onShuffleButtonPressed). trailer -> play this item's trailer(s). instantmix -> build an instant mix from the item (mirrors the instantMixButton).

list.title: module:ItemDetails.displayDirectorGenreNode
list.description: displayDirectorGenreNode: Add a node to the second info row, prepending a bullet divider if needed

list.title: module:ItemDetails.displayInfoNode
list.description: displayInfoNode: Add a node to the info group, prepending a bullet divider if needed

list.title: module:ItemDetails.displayRatingNodes
list.description: displayRatingNodes: Community + critic rating chips shared across every rateable item type's Row 1. Both are gated behind uiMoviesShowRatings and only render when the value is present (> 0), so it's safe to call for any type — types without a rating from the server simply show nothing. Values are passed explicitly (rather than reading item.* directly) so callers that need a parent-series fallback (Season) can resolve the value first.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.firstInteractiveDropdown
list.description: firstInteractiveDropdown: Returns the first visible, interactive TrackDropdown in left-to-right order, or invalid if none exist.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.focusButtonGroupChild
list.description: focusButtonGroupChild: Focus the button at the current buttonFocused index directly

list.title: module:ItemDetails.focusInteractiveDropdown
list.description: focusInteractiveDropdown: Walks the given candidate list and focuses the first dropdown that is interactive AND visible. The candidate order encodes both direction and wrap behavior; the currently-focused dropdown is always last so the nav falls back to it if nothing else is interactive (effectively: stay put).

list.title: module:ItemDetails.getButtonIndex
list.description: getButtonIndex: Find the index of a button by ID in the button group

list.title: module:ItemDetails.getHistory
list.description: getHistory: Format the air schedule/status string for a Series item Example output: "ABC" or "Fridays at 9:30 PM on NBC"

list.title: module:ItemDetails.getResumeButtonText
list.description: getResumeButtonText: Return "Resume S{n}E{n}" when season and episode numbers are known, otherwise fall back to plain "Resume".

list.title: module:ItemDetails.getStreamsForSelectedSource
list.description: getStreamsForSelectedSource: Pull the MediaStreams array from whichever media source matches m.top.selectedVideoStreamId. Falls back to mediaSources[0] when the selected id isn't found (e.g. first load before selection).

list.title: module:ItemDetails.isPlayAllType
list.description: True for item types whose Play builds a multi-item queue via an API call (handled by QuickPlayTask, not the single-item path below).

list.title: module:ItemDetails.launchQueueItemToPlay
list.description: Seed the queue with a single item and navigate to the play route. The router is idle (the user pressed a button on this settled view), so navigateTo won't hit the in-progress guard; PlayerHostView reads this queue on mount. :type/:id give the route a deep-link identity; the queue is the source of truth for what actually plays.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.loadFromRoute
list.description: Set itemType + itemId from a route's params. Setting itemId fires onItemIdChanged (the async loader). itemType is set first since the loader reads it.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.manageResumeButton
list.description: manageResumeButton: Add or remove Resume button based on playback position (non-Series types)

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onAudioDropdownSelection
list.description: Audio dropdown selection: updates selectedAudioStreamIndex and re-seeds the subtitle auto-selection (Smart mode depends on which audio is playing). If the user has already manually overridden the subtitle, we leave it alone.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onButtonGrpRendered
list.description: onButtonGrpRendered: Fires after the render thread settles the button group layout. Re-anchors the date label and repositions the logo. The track dropdown cluster lives inside itemDetails LayoutGroup and is positioned automatically by the layout engine, so it doesn't need to be re-placed here.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onClockMinuteChanged
list.description: onClockMinuteChanged: Dynamically update "Ends At" time when overhang clock minute changes.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onDeleteButtonPressed
list.description: Delete button. Confirm, then delete and leave the now-gone routed detail via the router. Scoped isDataReturned observer (see onWatchedButtonPressed).

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onDestroy
list.description: onDestroy: Full teardown releasing all resources before component removal Called automatically via JRScreen.beforeViewClose when sgRouter permanently closes this view.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onDetailNavButton
list.description: Single handler for every button on this detail's button group. Detail→detail nav (goToSeries / goToAlbum / goToArtist / goToChannel) navigates the router; playback buttons launch through the QueueManager / the play route; the rest run their render-thread action directly.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onDetailsLoaded
list.description: Callback when initial item details metadata arrives from the task

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onDropdownRequestDown
list.description: DOWN from any dropdown returns focus to the button group.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onDropdownRequestUp
list.description: Dropdown navigation: UP from any dropdown goes to description when in layout, else is consumed (no-op). parent-level UP handling reused so we don't have three identical handlers.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onExtrasItemSelected
list.description: Extras grid (cast / related / next-up) selection → navigate to its route, passing the rich node as context. Non-navigable items fall through to playback (quickPlayNode).

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onExtrasTargetTranslationYChanged
list.description: onExtrasTargetTranslationYChanged: Animate the RowList translation to the new target. Fired early by onKeyEvent in ExtrasRowList (at key press) so the animation runs in parallel with the RowList's floatingFocus animation, and confirmed by onRowItemFocused after focus lands.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onFavoriteToggleFailed
list.description: Reverts the favorite button to its pre-press state and surfaces an error toast.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onFirstEpisodeLoaded
list.description: onFirstEpisodeLoaded: Start playback of first episode for Series Play button

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onItemIdChanged
list.description: Triggered when itemId is set (route load) — kicks off async data load

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onLogoLoadStatusChanged
list.description: onLogoLoadStatusChanged: Position logo to the right, with its bottom just above the date label. When the date label is hidden, anchors to where the date label would be. Images smaller than LOGO_MIN_DISPLAY_HEIGHT are scaled up while preserving aspect ratio, unless the logo is very wide/flat, in which case LOGO_MAX_DISPLAY_WIDTH takes precedence.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onLyricsLoaded
list.description: onLyricsLoaded: Show lyrics in the description area once the async fetch completes (Audio only)

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onNextUpEpisodeChanged
list.description: nextUpEpisodeChanged: For Series — create/remove Resume button based on nextUpEpisode. On first load, replaces the LoadingButton placeholder from setupButtons(). On refresh, inserts/updates/removes the Resume button directly.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onPersonHasMediaChanged
list.description: onPersonHasMediaChanged: Show or hide the Shuffle button once the person extras chain completes. On first load, replaces the LoadingButton placeholder from setupButtons(). On refresh, inserts/removes the Shuffle button directly. Fires via alwaysNotify so it triggers even when personHasMedia stays the same value (e.g. on refresh).

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onPlaylistContentKindChanged
list.description:
onPlaylistContentKindChanged: Drives the Watched button and item-count label based on the confirmed content kind of the playlist ("video" / "audio" / "mixed" / "unknown"). Fires via alwaysNotify so it triggers even when the kind is unchanged (e.g. on refresh).

Watched button: only shown for "video" (has at least one video-type item). Label: "Tracks" only when positively confirmed "audio" (all items are Audio type). All other kinds — including "unknown" (load failure) and "mixed" — stay "Items".

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onQuickPlayLaunch
list.description: Forward a non-navigable extras selection to the playback launcher.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onRecordButtonPressed
list.description: Record / cancel-record a Live TV program. Owns the RecordProgramTask on this render thread.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onRefreshDetailsLoaded
list.description: Callback when refreshed item details metadata arrives

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onRefreshExtrasData
list.description: onRefreshExtrasData: Reload all extras rows when the user explicitly presses Refresh.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onRefreshItemDetailsData
list.description: Triggered by refreshItemDetailsData field toggle (return from playback, refresh button). Refreshes silently in the background — no spinner, no input blocking. The UI already has data from the initial load so the user can navigate freely.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onRefreshResumeData
list.description: Triggered by refreshResumeData field toggle (watched toggle on Series). Re-fetches ONLY the next-up/resume episode — no full rebuild, no setupButtons(). The existing onNextUpEpisodeChanged() handles adding/removing/updating the resume button.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onRouteUpdate
list.description: The router reuses this view (no remount) when navigating to the SAME details path it's already showing — e.g. a cast of the item you're already on, after a player above it was popped. m.top.route is the new route by now, so re-run the deep-link check (its route.id dedup makes it a one-shot per navigation). Non-deep-link same-path updates are no-ops.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onScreenShown
list.description: onScreenShown: Callback when view is presented on screen

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onSeasonSeriesDataLoaded
list.description: onSeasonSeriesDataLoaded: Update Season info rows and logo with parent series metadata

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onSeriesResumeLoaded
list.description: onSeriesResumeLoaded: Set nextUpEpisode from seriesResume task result to show/hide the Resume button

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onShuffleButtonPressed
list.description: Shuffle button. Routes by item type so new types can be added. PhotoAlbum launches a random slideshow; everything else resolves a randomized item set via GetShuffleItemsTask on the QueueManager.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onSubtitleDropdownSelection
list.description: Subtitle dropdown selection: marks user override so subsequent audio changes don't clobber the pick, then stores the new index. Action "-1" = Off.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onTrackDropdownFocusChanged
list.description: onTrackDropdownFocusChanged: Observer wired to each TrackDropdown's focusedChild. Remembers the last dropdown that held focus so the UP-from-buttons handler can restore the previous slot on re-entry instead of always defaulting to video.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onTrackDropdownMenuOpenChanged
list.description: onTrackDropdownMenuOpenChanged: Observer wired to each TrackDropdown's menuOpen. Toggles the full-screen dimmer iff ANY dropdown's menu is currently open. The trackCluster (titles + triggers + the open menu itself) renders ABOVE the dimmer in XML sibling order, so only the screen content BEHIND the cluster dims — category titles and trigger labels stay full-brightness as the user's "you are here" cue while picking a track.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onVideoDropdownFocusExit
list.description: LEFT/RIGHT between dropdowns — each dropdown has its own exit handler so we know which slot we came from and can target the correct neighbor, skipping non-interactive slots.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onVideoDropdownSelection
list.description: Video dropdown selection fires when the user picks a different MediaSource (alternate version). We swap in the new source's streams, re-run audio auto-select, and rebuild the audio/subtitle dropdowns against the new source. Focus stays on the video dropdown.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onWatchChannelButtonPressed
list.description: Watch a Live TV channel (TvChannel detail, or the channel a Program airs on) — single-item launch via the play route.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onWatchedButtonPressed
list.description: Watched button. Movie/Episode toggle immediately; a Series confirms first (marking all episodes can affect hundreds). The confirmation result is read off the SceneManager via a SCOPED isDataReturned observer (the proven settings.bs pattern). On confirm we call toggleWatched(), which re-reads the button state to pick mark vs unmark.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onWatchedConfirmResponse
list.description: Series mark-all-watched confirmation result.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.onWatchedToggleFailed
list.description: Reverts the watched button to its pre-press state, clears the resume button's loading state, and surfaces an error toast.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.pickTrackDropdownFromButtons
list.description:
pickTrackDropdownFromButtons: Chooses which TrackDropdown to focus when UP is pressed from the button group. Ignores m.lastFocusedDropdown by design — UP-from-buttons should ALWAYS land on the dropdown visually above the currently focused button, not on wherever the user was last time. Each slot spans 2 buttons: slot 1 sits above buttons [0,1], slot 2 above [2,3], slot 3 above [4,5,...]. Widens outward to the nearest interactive slot if the preferred one is static/hidden.

CRITICAL: guards on m.trackCluster.visible — when the item type has no media tracks (Series/Person/BoxSet/etc.), the cluster is hidden but the individual dropdown nodes may still carry stale visible=true / isInteractive=true from a previously-loaded item. Without this guard, focus would move to an invisible stale dropdown when the user presses UP on those item types.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.pickTrackDropdownFromDescription
list.description: pickTrackDropdownFromDescription: Chooses which TrackDropdown to focus when DOWN is pressed from the description. Restores m.lastFocusedDropdown if still usable so the user returns to wherever they were before moving up. Falls back to the leftmost interactive dropdown (video → audio → subtitles) for the first-ever entry. Also guards on m.trackCluster.visible to match pickTrackDropdownFromButtons above.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.playFromDeepLink
list.description: Run the deep-link action on this already-loaded item in response to a runtime cast that landed while the user is ALREADY on its details (replayRoute.replayDeepLinkRuntime) — no navigation, so no Home flash and no re-mount. The router is idle here, so any navigateTo the action triggers succeeds directly (no NavigationEnd defer needed). action is the new cast's intent (the cached view's m.deepLinkAction is from its original mount and may be stale).

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateDescriptionGroup
list.description: populateDescriptionGroup: Set FocusableOverview text with tagline and overview

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroup
list.description: populateInfoGroup: Dispatch to type-specific info row builder

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupAudio
list.description: populateInfoGroupAudio: Info rows for Audio (song) Row 1: Track N · Disc N (only when disc > 1) · Runtime · Ends At Row 2: Album name · Album artist

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupBoxSet
list.description: populateInfoGroupBoxSet: Info rows for BoxSet (movie collection) Row 1: Year · Official Rating · Community Rating · Critic Rating · N Movies Row 2: Genres · Studio

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupEpisode
list.description: populateInfoGroupEpisode: Info rows for Episode Row 1: Air Date · Official Rating · Community Rating · Critic Rating · Runtime · Ends At Row 2: Series name + "S{n}E{n}"

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupMovie
list.description: populateInfoGroupMovie: Info rows for Movie, Video, Recording Row 1: Year · Official Rating · Community Rating · Critic Rating · Runtime · Ends At Row 2: Genres · Director(s)

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupMusicAlbum
list.description: populateInfoGroupMusicAlbum: Info rows for MusicAlbum Row 1: Year · Runtime · Ends At Row 2: Album Artist · Genres · N Tracks

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupMusicArtist
list.description: populateInfoGroupMusicArtist: Info rows for MusicArtist Row 1: N Albums · N Songs · Year Row 2: Genres

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupMusicVideo
list.description: populateInfoGroupMusicVideo: Info rows for MusicVideo Row 1: Year · Official Rating · Community Rating · Critic Rating · Runtime · Ends At Row 2: Genres · Artist(s)

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupPerson
list.description: populateInfoGroupPerson: Info rows for Person Row 1: {birthDate} [- {deathDate}] · {n} years old Living: Jan 1, 1980 · 45 years old Deceased: Jan 1, 1920 - Dec 31, 1980 · 60 years old Row 2: {n} Movie(s) [· {n} Episode(s)] — omitted entirely if both counts are 0

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupPhoto
list.description: populateInfoGroupPhoto: Info rows for Photo Row 1: Resolution ("WxH") · Camera ("Make Model") · Production Year Row 2: Album name (if present)

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupPhotoAlbum
list.description: populateInfoGroupPhotoAlbum: Info rows for PhotoAlbum Row 1: Photo count

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupPlaylist
list.description: populateInfoGroupPlaylist: Info rows for Playlist Row 1: N Items · Runtime · Ends At Row 2: Genres The item count label starts as "Items" and is swapped to "Tracks" by onPlaylistContentKindChanged() once the extras chain confirms the playlist is all-audio.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupProgram
list.description: populateInfoGroupProgram: Info rows for Program (Live TV EPG entry) Row 1: Year · CH X - ChanName · OfficialRating · CommunityRating · CriticRating · S#E# · Premiere/Repeat · Runtime · BroadcastTime · Ends At Row 2: Genres (or category flags)

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupRecording
list.description: populateInfoGroupRecording: Info rows for Recording (Live TV recorded content) Row 1: PremiereYear · OfficialRating · CommunityRating · CriticRating · S#E# · Runtime · Ends At · CH X - ChanName · Recorded: Date Row 2: Genres

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupSeason
list.description: populateInfoGroupSeason: Info rows for Season Row 1: Year · Official Rating (series) · Community Rating (series) · Critic Rating (series) · Avg episode runtime (series) · Ends At Row 2: Series name · Episode count · Studio

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupSeries
list.description: populateInfoGroupSeries: Info rows for Series Row 1: Year Range · Official Rating · Community Rating · Critic Rating · Runtime · Ends At (only when avg episode runtime is available) Row 2: Genres · Air schedule/Status

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateInfoGroupTvChannel
list.description: populateInfoGroupTvChannel: Info rows for TvChannel (Live TV) Displays metadata from the current program (the channel is a vessel for what's airing). Row 1: CH N · OfficialRating · CommunityRating · CriticRating · S#E# · Program Name · Runtime · BroadcastTime · Ends At Row 2: Genres (or category flags)

list.title: module:ItemDetails.populateTrackDropdowns
list.description: populateTrackDropdowns: Build the Video/Audio/Subtitle dropdown items from the current media source list and stream list for the given item type, then toggle the cluster + title row visibility. Seeds m.top.selectedSubtitleStreamIndex from findDefaultSubtitleStreamIndex() on first load so the initial trigger label reflects what playback will actually use.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.removeResumeButtonWithFocus
list.description: removeResumeButtonWithFocus: Remove resume button while preserving focus position

list.title: module:ItemDetails.removeResumeLoadingButton
list.description: removeResumeLoadingButton: Remove the resume LoadingButton placeholder if still present. Called when async data confirms no resume episode exists (nextUpEpisode stays invalid).

list.title: module:ItemDetails.resolveGoToArtistId
list.description: Resolve the artist id for goToArtist: prefer the primary album artist, fall back to the single artist item.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.resumeSeriesNextUp
list.description: Series Resume plays the next-up episode (a single item) from its saved position, via the play route.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.setDateAdded
list.description: setDateAdded: Set date added label text and position at bottom right corner

list.title: module:ItemDetails.setDescriptionInLayout
list.description: setDescriptionInLayout: Add or remove itemDescription from the itemDetails LayoutGroup. Invisible nodes still take up space in RSG LayoutGroups, so reparenting is required to truly eliminate spacing when there is no description text.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.setItemLogo
list.description:
setItemLogo: Set the item logo image URL if available. Fallback chains by type — every chain ends in a typed placeholder via getPlaceholderImagePath() so the logo slot never goes blank, matching the pattern Person and MusicArtist always used:

  • Movie/Series/BoxSet: Logo → primaryImageTag (poster, compact size) → placeholder
  • Episode/Season/Recording: primaryImageTag → parentLogoImageTag (series logo) → seriesPrimaryImageTag → placeholder
  • Video/MusicVideo: primaryImageTag → placeholder
  • MusicAlbum / Playlist / Photo / PhotoAlbum / TvChannel: primaryImageTag → placeholder
  • Program: primaryImageTag → channel logo → placeholder
  • Audio: primaryImageTag → album art → placeholder
  • Person / MusicArtist: primaryImageTag (portrait) → placeholder

list.title: module:ItemDetails.setTracksInLayout
list.description: setTracksInLayout: Add or remove the 62px itemTracks spacer from the itemDetails LayoutGroup. When a track cluster is applicable to the current item, the spacer stays in the layout and pushes description bottom up to y=701 so the trackCluster sibling can sit at y=713 with 12px breathing pad above and 18px below between the description and the button group. For item types without any media tracks (Series/Person/etc.), the spacer is removed and description bottom drops back to y=775 (matching main's behavior — no cluster, no padding, no regression).

list.title: module:ItemDetails.setupButtons
list.description: setupButtons: Build the complete button set for the given item type. Clears all existing buttons and adds the correct sync buttons in order. Loading placeholders are added for async buttons (Series Resume, Person Shuffle) so the button row renders at its final width immediately. The async handlers replace or remove the placeholders when data arrives. Called on every itemContentChanged — the button list is rebuilt from scratch each time.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.startPlayFromDetail
list.description: Build the single-item play queue from this detail's own state and navigate to the play route. The router is idle (the user pressed a button on this settled view), so navigateTo won't hit the in-progress guard, and the PlayerHostView reads this queue on mount.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.toggleFavorite
list.description: callFunc entry — toggles the favorite state of the current item. Invoked from main.bs's button router; because callFunc rendezvouses to this render-thread-owned node, the body (and the fetchAsync().then() promise delivery + auto-abandon) runs on this component's render thread, where the named-observer adapter works. This is the Phase-3c canonical "main thread → render thread delegation" pattern — see docs/dev/promises.md. It moves the favorite toggle off main.bs's god-loop (was submitApiRequest + isDone branch + handler).

list.title: module:ItemDetails.toggleWatched
list.description: callFunc entry — toggles the watched/played state of the current item. Invoked from main.bs's button router; Movie/Episode call it directly, while Series route through the "mark all episodes" confirmation dialog first (main.bs keeps that dialog routing and re-invokes this on confirm — #550 sgRouter territory). Because callFunc rendezvouses to this render-thread-owned node, the body (and the fetchAsync().then() promise delivery + auto-abandon) runs on this component's render thread, where the named-observer adapter works. This is the deferred twin of toggleFavorite() — the Phase-3c "main thread → render thread delegation" pattern (see docs/dev/promises.md). It moves the LAST raw submitApiRequest + isDone flow off main.bs's god-loop.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.triggerTextForSelection
list.description: triggerTextForSelection: Look up the currently-selected item's display title.

list.title: module:ItemDetails.updateItemDetailsAnimationTarget
list.description: updateItemDetailsAnimationTarget: Slide itemDetails so the bottom of itemInfoRows sits just above the extras pane. boundingRect() on a child returns LOCAL coords relative to the parent's translation point, so we use translation[1] (not boundingRect().y) as the screen origin. Called just-in-time from the DOWN key handler to guarantee the layout is fully settled (text wrapping complete) before measuring. Eager calculation via renderTracking races with async text layout, producing stale targets — especially for long descriptions.

list.title: module:ItemGridOptions.buttonFocusChanged
list.description: Switch menu shown when button focus changes

list.title: module:ItemGridOptions.isFilterMenuDataValid
list.description: Check if data for Filter Menu is valid

list.title: module:JRButtonGroup.center
list.description: center the button group horizontally NOTE: this centers using the entire screen width, not the parent bounding rect

list.title: module:JRButtonGroup.doCentering
list.description: Actually perform the centering calculation

list.title: module:JRButtonGroup.onChildReady
list.description: Called when a child button becomes ready

list.title: module:JRButtons.focusChanged
list.description: Change opacity of the highlighted menu item based on focus

list.title: module:JRButtons.highlightSelected
list.description: Highlight selected menu option

list.title: module:JRButtons.onSelectedIndexChanged
list.description: When Selected Index set, ensure it is the one Focused

list.title: module:JRGroup.onDestroy
list.description:
Base codebehind for JRGroup — the parent of every sub-panel / dialog (and, transitively, every JRScreen). Kept intentionally minimal: it exists only to provide the onDestroy() floor below.

Floor for JRGroup subclasses (panels/dialogs) that DON'T override onDestroy(): abandon any pending fetchAsync promises so a late pool response can't fire a callback into the destroyed node. Subclasses that DO override onDestroy() get this via the auto-abandon-promises BSC plugin (which injects abandonApiPromises() into their own onDestroy) — SG component onDestroy does not chain to this base, so the call can't rely on inheritance alone.

list.title: module:JROverhang.positionTabBar
